tasks.title: Loot Choice after expedition is wonky
tasks.summary:
Loot recap shows the first item loot and ask to Bring this back or leave the rest. So user does not know which other loots are available before making a decision, and its bugged as right now "bring this back" saves the loot and moves to the next, effectively allowing user to save all loot in the end.

The intended result would be a carousel (can be animation less for now) of the available loots, have the user browse and then pick one. Put a confirmation msg that the other loots will be discarded.
Max loot in the carousel/to be saved can come from quest/hero passives/tavern upgrades/Contract Plan.

acceptance.text: Show all pending loot in a browseable choice surface before the player commits.
Let the player select up to the current recovery capacity, then confirm that unselected loot is discarded.
Prevent keeping every pending item through repeated Bring this back actions when capacity is lower than the drop count.
Keep recovery capacity sources compatible with quest, hero passive, facility, and contract-plan bonuses.
Add or update focused tests for grouped pending-loot selection and discard behavior.

notes: 2026-06-25: Started implementation; treating this as a grouped pending-loot selection flow with confirmation.

2026-06-25: Reward Loot now uses a grouped browse/select/confirm flow; selected loot is committed atomically up to recovery capacity and unselected pending loot is discarded. Tests, assembleDebug, lintDebug, and diff check passed.

tasks.title: Special Contracts: make contract plans a first-class activity lane
tasks.summary: Turn Special Contracts into a limited high-stakes activity lane. The player spends one stored contract to launch a Special Contract, contracts can be looted or earned passively/through achievements with no storage cap, and the payoff should be XXL enough to feel worth the item cost.

acceptance.text: List the available contract clause on each compatible quest in the Special Contracts region.
Add a stored Special Contract resource with no upper storage limit.
Award Special Contracts from at least one active source and one passive or achievement source.
Require and consume one Special Contract when launching a Special Contract plan or lane activity.
Keep standard/default quest plans free so normal questing never stalls behind contracts.
Make Special Contract rewards visibly XXL through stronger gold/loot/ticket payoff than normal repeats.
Show contract inventory, item cost, and locked/empty state in Expedition Prep now, and in the Quest Activity Board when BV-027 lands.
Keep Expedition Prep as the final confirmation surface and avoid duplicating launch logic.
Add persistence and tests for earning, storing without cap, consuming, and reward payoff.

notes: 2026-06-25: Split from the broader Quest Activity Board idea so contracts can get their own UX pass.

2026-06-25: User refined Special Contracts into a limited consumable action: spend 1 stored contract to launch, contracts come from loot/passive/achievements, storage has no cap, and rewards should be XXL.

tasks.title: Guild hub: pixel-perfect hotspot mask pipeline
tasks.summary: Replace the temporary polygon/debug-style Guild hub interaction layer with a production pixel-perfect hotspot and highlight pipeline. The current BV-018 hub is acceptable as a functional first pass, but final interaction should be driven by art-authored masks instead of hand-tuned polygons.

acceptance.text: Create a guild_hub_hotspot_mask.png at the exact same dimensions as the base hub artwork, using one flat unique color per action: Charter/Achievements, Core Crew, Quests/Expedition, Loot, Facilities/Upgrades; transparent or black pixels mean no action.

Create production highlight assets per hotspot, or derive highlight/outline rendering from the hotspot mask, so selected/clickable objects are highlighted on their exact silhouette instead of with approximate polygons or floating markers.
Replace GuildScreen polygon hit testing with rendered-image coordinate mapping plus mask color lookup, preserving aspect-ratio letterboxing/cropping behavior across phone sizes.
Keep HUD band, bottom drawer band, central floor, and decorative props non-interactive unless explicitly assigned a future feature.
Document the mask palette, action mapping, dimensions, and export rules so future hub art can be rebuilt from source without guessing.
Add tests for color-to-action mapping, coordinate conversion, dead zones, and at least one emulator smoke capture proving the hub highlights and taps line up.

notes: 2026-06-26: Created from Guild hub implementation review. User accepted the current BV-018 version as a temporary functional pass, but the final solution should be pixel-perfect: base art plus hotspot mask plus highlight masks/derived outlines. Do not spend more time polishing the temporary polygon highlight beyond keeping it usable.

2026-06-26: Added exact-size guild_hub_hotspot_mask.png, documented the palette/export pipeline, replaced polygon taps with mask color lookup and mask-derived highlights, and added mapping/dead-zone tests. Verified focused test, test/assembleDebug/lintDebug, installDebug, mask palette check, and emulator smoke with Loot hotspot tap plus central-floor dead-zone check.

Captured all five hotspot selections under artifacts/screenshots/bv035 and verified test, assembleDebug, lintDebug, and installDebug.

tasks.title: Guild Home: hotspot discoverability and first-use guidance
tasks.summary: The hub is now clickable, but a new player may not immediately know that the charter board, crew desk, quest table, loot chest, and facilities rack are the main actions. Add lightweight affordances without turning the screen back into a text menu.

acceptance.text: Add a restrained first-use hint or pulse that teaches the player to tap guild landmarks.
Show short landmark labels or hints only when helpful, then reduce them after the player successfully taps hotspots.
Persist or derive whether the hint has already been satisfied without creating noisy save-state risk.
Localize visible hint copy in EN and FR.

notes: 2026-06-26: Created from screenshot review. The art has readable landmarks, but the interaction model needs a gentle onboarding affordance.

tasks.title: Guild Home: bottom navigation icon polish
tasks.summary: The bottom nav currently uses letter placeholders inside chips, which weakens the otherwise bespoke pixel-art presentation. Replace placeholder letters with polished tab icons and a quieter active state that still works in portrait.

acceptance.text: Replace G/Q/H/L/U letter placeholders with readable themed icons or pixel assets for Guild, Quests, Heroes, Loot, and Upgrades.
Tune selected/unselected tab states so the active tab is clear without adding bulky cards over the hub.
Verify EN/FR labels do not clip and the nav clears the Android navigation bar/home indicator area.
Add screenshot evidence for the Guild tab and at least one non-Guild tab after the icon pass.

notes: 2026-06-26: Created from screenshot review. The bottom nav typography and letter chips read like placeholders next to the hub art.

tasks.title: Guild Home: responsive safe-area and device-frame QA
tasks.summary: The screenshot shows the Guild Home inside a phone frame with tight status-bar, bottom-nav, and card overlays. Create a deliberate QA pass for portrait device bounds so the hub art, HUD, drawer, and navigation do not overlap awkwardly across common sizes.

acceptance.text: Define minimum layout constraints for the Guild Home at small, standard, and tall portrait sizes.
Verify status bar, cutout/camera area, bottom navigation, home indicator, HUD, and drawer do not overlap content incoherently.
Capture emulator screenshots for at least two portrait resolutions and include them under artifacts/screenshots.
Add focused layout or mapping tests where feasible so future hub changes do not regress safe-area behavior.

notes: 2026-06-26: Created from screenshot review. The Guild Home needs explicit portrait-device QA now that it is becoming the main visual surface.

tasks.title: Guild Home: stateful landmark storytelling
tasks.summary: The hub art is attractive but mostly static. Make each landmark communicate useful game state so the player can read what needs attention before opening a drawer or tab.

acceptance.text: Show pending loot or recovery attention on the Loot Cache landmark without relying only on drawer text.
Show claimable achievements or charter progress on the Guild Charter landmark.
Show active/running/result-ready quest state on the Quest Table landmark.
Show upgrade availability or facility attention on the Facilities landmark.
Keep state indicators subtle, localized where text is visible, and aligned to the mask-defined landmarks.

notes: 2026-06-26: Created from screenshot review. The hub should eventually answer what needs attention at a glance, not only after the player taps a hotspot.
